parameters.name: xord
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: This allows you to specify the order in which the changes to your will take place. Changing the Transform Order will change where things go much the same way as going a block and turning east gets you

parameters.name: rord
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description: This allows you to set the transform order for the 's rotations. As with transform order (above), changing the order in which the 's rotations take place will alter the 's final position.	A Rotation o

parameters.name: scale
parameters.label: Uniform Scale
parameters.description: This field allows you to change the size of an uniformly along the three axes.

parameters.name: parentxformsrc
parameters.label: Parent Transform Source
parameters.description: Select what position is used as the transform source for this obejct. Can be one of "Parent (Hierarchy)", "Specify Parent Object", or "World Origin".

parameters.name: parentobject
parameters.label: Parent Object
parameters.description: Allows the location of the object to be constrained to any other object whose path is specified in this parameter.

parameters.name: lookat
parameters.label: Look At
parameters.description: Allows you to orient this by naming another 3D you would like it to , or point to. Once you have designated this to look at, it will continue to face that , even if you move it. This is useful if, for

parameters.name: forwarddir
parameters.label: Forward Direction
parameters.description: Sets which axis and direction is considered the forward direction.

parameters.name: lookup
parameters.label: Look At Up Vector
parameters.description: When specifying a , it is possible to specify an up vector for the lookat. Without using an up vector, it is possible to get poor animation when the lookat , for example, passes through the Y axis of

parameters.name: pathsop
parameters.label: Path SOP
parameters.description: Names the that functions as the path you want this to move along. For instance, you can name a that provides a path for the camera to follow.

parameters.name: roll
parameters.label: Roll
parameters.description: Using the angle control you can specify a 's rotation as it animates along the path.

parameters.name: pos
parameters.label: Position
parameters.description: This parameter lets you specify the of the along the path. The values you can enter for this parameter range from to , where equals the starting point and equals the end point of the path. The value s

parameters.name: pathorient
parameters.label: Orient along Path
parameters.description: If this option is selected, the will be oriented along the path. The positive Z axis of the will be pointing down the path.

parameters.name: bank
parameters.label: Auto-Bank Factor
parameters.description: The rolls the based on the curvature of the path at its current position. To turn off auto-banking, set the bank scale to .

parameters.name: pxform
parameters.label: Apply Pre-Transform
parameters.description: Enables the transformation on this page.

parameters.name: pxord
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: Refer to the documentation on Xform page for more information.

parameters.name: prord
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description: Refer to the documentation on Xform page for more information.

parameters.name: pscale
parameters.label: Uniform Scale
parameters.description: Refer to the documentation on Xform page for more information.

parameters.name: preset
parameters.label: Reset Transform
parameters.description: This button will reset this page's transform so it has no translate/rotate/scale.

parameters.name: pcommit
parameters.label: Commit to Main Transform
parameters.description: This button will copy the transform from this page to the main Xform page, and reset this page's transform.

parameters.name: xformmatrixop
parameters.label: Xform Matrix/CHOP/DAT
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to transform using a 4x4 matrix directly. For information on ways to specify a matrix directly, refer to the page. This transform will be applied after the regular Pre-Trans

parameters.name: dimmer
parameters.label: Dimmer
parameters.description: This parameter changes the intensity of the light without affecting its hue. Lights with Dimmer intensity below 0.001 are ignored. This optimization allows lights that are set to 0.0 to not be calcula

parameters.name: lighttype
parameters.label: Light Type
parameters.description: Specifies the type of light.

parameters.name: coneangle
parameters.label: Cone Angle
parameters.description: This specifies the angle within which the light remains at full intensity. Decreasing the cone angle to between ten and forty degrees focuses the beam to spotlight proportions.

parameters.name: conedelta
parameters.label: Cone Delta
parameters.description: This value, in degrees, represents the angle outside the cone angle through which the light intensity drops from its maximum to zero. Beyond this area, no more light is cast.

parameters.name: coneroll
parameters.label: Cone Rolloff
parameters.description: This parameter (a value between one and ten) defines how gently or suddenly the amount of light decreases between full intensity and zero intensity within the Cone Delta area.

parameters.name: attenuated
parameters.label: Distance-Attenuated
parameters.description: Turn on this checkbox to enable distance-based attenuation of the light.

parameters.name: attenuationstart
parameters.label: Attenuation Start
parameters.description: The distance from the light source where the light attenuation begins.

parameters.name: attenuationend
parameters.label: Attenuation End
parameters.description: The distance from the light source where the light attenuation ends (i.e., no light radiates beyond this point).

parameters.name: attenuationexp
parameters.label: Attenuation Rolloff
parameters.description: Controls how the light fades off between the Attenuation Start and End points.

parameters.name: projmap
parameters.label: Projector Map
parameters.description: The path to a used for the light's projector map.

parameters.name: projmapextendu
parameters.label: Extend U
parameters.description: Sets the extend conditions for the Projector Map texture.

parameters.name: projmapextendv
parameters.label: Extend V
parameters.description: Sets the extend conditions for the Projector Map texture.

parameters.name: projmapextendw
parameters.label: Extend W
parameters.description: Sets the extend conditions for the Projector Map texture.

parameters.name: projmapfilter
parameters.label: Filter
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: projmapanisotropy
parameters.label: Anisotropic Filter
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: projmapmode
parameters.label: Projector Map Mode
parameters.description: Specify how the projection map is applied

parameters.name: projangle
parameters.label: Projector Angle
parameters.description: Specifies the cone angle spread of the projector map, similar to how the Cone Angle parameter works for Cone Lights.

parameters.name: frontfacelit
parameters.label: Polygon Front Faces
parameters.description: Controls how the polygon's normal is used to light the front face of the polygon. For more information refer to the article.

parameters.name: backfacelit
parameters.label: Polygon Back Faces
parameters.description: Controls how the polygon's normal is used to light the back face of the polygon. For more information refer to the article.

parameters.name: shadowtype
parameters.label: Shadow Type
parameters.description: Sets the type of shadows cast by the light.

parameters.name: shadowcasters
parameters.label: Shadow Casters
parameters.description: The that will cast shadows from this light.

parameters.name: maxshadowsoftness
parameters.label: Max Shadow Softness
parameters.description: Fine tuning for the shadow's software when using Soft or Custom shadows.

parameters.name: filtersamples
parameters.label: Filter Samples
parameters.description: Controls how many samples to look up into the shadow map for each pixel when doing soft shadows.

parameters.name: searchsteps
parameters.label: Search Steps
parameters.description: Controls how many steps to take to search for occlusion when doing soft shadows.

parameters.name: polygonoffsetfactor
parameters.label: Polygon Offset Factor
parameters.description: Adds an offset to the Z value that depends on how sloped the surface is to the viewer when rendering the shadow map. Helps avoid z-fighting artifacts.

parameters.name: polygonoffsetunits
parameters.label: Polygon Offset Units
parameters.description: Adds a constant offset to the Z value when rendering the shadow map. Helps avoid z-fighting artifacts.

parameters.name: shadowmap
parameters.label: Custom Shadow Map
parameters.description: The path to a used for the light's shadow map.  See also .

parameters.name: projection
parameters.label: Projection
parameters.description: A pop-up menu lets you choose the projection type.

parameters.name: aspectcorrect
parameters.label: Aspect Correct Projection
parameters.description: Keeps the aspect ratio of the view correct when using the light as a camera to look through.

parameters.name: orthowidth
parameters.label: Ortho Width
parameters.description: Only active if Orthographic is chosen from the Projection pop-up menu. This specifies the width of the orthographic projection.

parameters.name: useconeforfov
parameters.label: Use Cone Angle/Delta for FOV
parameters.description: If the light is set to Cone Light type, enabling this option sets the FOV using the Cone Angle and Cone Delta parameters on the Light parameter page.

parameters.name: viewanglemethod
parameters.label: Viewing Angle Method
parameters.description: This menu determines which method is used to define the camera's angle of view.

parameters.name: fov
parameters.label: FOV Angle
parameters.description: The field of view (FOV) angle is the angular extend of the scene imaged by the camera.

parameters.name: focal
parameters.label: Focal Length
parameters.description: The parameter sets the focal length of the lens, zooming in and out. Perspective is flattened or exaggerated depending on focal length. Some interesting distortion effects can be acheived with this pa

parameters.name: aperture
parameters.label: Aperture
parameters.description: This value relates to the area through which light can pass for the camera.

parameters.name: near
parameters.label: Near
parameters.description: This control allows you to designate the near clipping planes. Geometry closer to the lens than these distances will not be visible.

parameters.name: far
parameters.label: Far
parameters.description: This control allows you to designate the far clipping planes. Geometry further away from the lens than these distances will not be visible.

parameters.name: projmatrixop
parameters.label: Proj Matrix/CHOP/DAT
parameters.description: When Custom Projection Matrix is selected, this parameters should be filled in with either a or a with a custom 4x4 projection matrix. If a is used the first sample of the first 16 channels of the are

parameters.name: customproj
parameters.label: Custom Projection GLSL DAT
parameters.description: Takes a containing a GLSL shader to specify custom projection functions. You must provide two functions in this shader. Both functions must be provided and return correct results for your rendering to

parameters.name: material
parameters.label: Material
parameters.description: Selects a to apply to the geometry inside.

parameters.name: render
parameters.label: Render
parameters.description: Whether the 's geometry is visible in the . This parameter works in conjunction (logical AND) with the 's .

parameters.name: drawpriority
parameters.label: Draw Priority
parameters.description: Determines the order in which the Components are drawn. Smaller values get drawn after larger values. The value is compared with other Components in the same parent , or if the is the top level one li

parameters.name: pickpriority
parameters.label: Pick Priority
parameters.description: When using a or a , there is an option to have a 'Search Area'. If multiple objects are found within the search area, the pick priority can be used to select one object over another. A higher value wi

parameters.name: reinitextensions
parameters.label: Re-Init Extensions
parameters.description: Recompile all extension objects. Normally extension objects are compiled only when they are referenced and their definitions have changed.

parameters.name: initextonstart
parameters.label: Init Extensions On Start
parameters.description: Perform a Re-Init automatically when TouchDEsigner Starts

parameters.name: ext
parameters.label: Extension
parameters.description: Sequence of info for creating extensions on this component

parameters.name: ext0object
parameters.label: Object
parameters.description: A number of class instances that can be attached to the component.

parameters.name: ext0name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: Optional name to search by, instead of the instance class name.

parameters.name: ext0promote
parameters.label: Promote
parameters.description: Controls whether or not the extensions are visible directly at the component level, or must be accessed through the member.  Example: vs

parameters.name: parentshortcut
parameters.label: Parent Shortcut
parameters.description: Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as the path to that component. See .

parameters.name: opshortcut
parameters.label: Global OP Shortcut
parameters.description: Specifies a name you can use anywhere at all as the path to that component. See .

parameters.name: iop
parameters.label: Internal OP
parameters.description: Sequence header for internal operators.

parameters.name: iop0shortcut
parameters.label: Shortcut
parameters.description: Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as a path to "Internal " below. See .

parameters.name: iop0op
parameters.label: OP
parameters.description: The path to the Internal OP inside this component. See .

parameters.name: nodeview
parameters.label: Node View
parameters.description: Determines what is displayed in the node viewer, also known as the . Some options will not be available depending on the type ( , , Misc.)

parameters.name: opviewer
parameters.label: Operator Viewer
parameters.description: Select which operator's node viewer to use when the Node View parameter above is set to Operator .

parameters.name: enablecloning
parameters.label: Enable Cloning
parameters.description: Control if the OP should be actively cloneing. Turning this off causes this node to stop cloning it's 'Clone Master'.

parameters.name: enablecloningpulse
parameters.label: Enable Cloning Pulse
parameters.description: Instantaneously clone the contents.

parameters.name: clone
parameters.label: Clone Master
parameters.description: to a component used as the Master .

parameters.name: loadondemand
parameters.label: Load on Demand
parameters.description: Loads the component into memory only when required. Good to use for components that are not always used in the project.

parameters.name: enableexternaltox
parameters.label: Enable External .tox
parameters.description: When on (default), the external file will be loaded when the starts and the contents of the will match that of the external . This can be turned off to avoid loading from the referenced external on st

parameters.name: enableexternaltoxpulse
parameters.label: Enable External .tox Pulse
parameters.description: This button will re-load from the external file (if present).

parameters.name: externaltox
parameters.label: External .tox Path
parameters.description: to a file on disk which will source the component's contents upon start of a . This allows for components to contain networks that can be updated independently. If the file can not be found, whatever

parameters.name: reloadcustom
parameters.label: Reload Custom Parameters
parameters.description: When this checkbox is enabled, the values of the component's are reloaded when the is reloaded. This only affects top-level parameters on the component, all parameters on nodes inside the component ar

parameters.name: reloadbuiltin
parameters.label: Reload Built-In Parameters
parameters.description: When this checkbox is enabled, the values of the component's built-in parameters are reloaded when the is reloaded. This only affects top-level parameters on the component, all parameters on nodes ins

parameters.name: savebackup
parameters.label: Save Backup of External
parameters.description: When this checkbox is enabled, a backup copy of the component specified by the External parameter is saved in the file.  This backup copy will be used if the External can not be found. This may happen

parameters.name: subcompname
parameters.label: Sub-Component to Load
parameters.description: When loading from an External file, this option allows you to reach into the and pull out a and make that the top-level , ignoring everything else in the file (except for the contents of that ). For e

parameters.name: relpath
parameters.label: Relative File Path Behavior
parameters.description: Set whether the child file paths within this are relative to the itself or the , or inherit from parent.

parameters.name: cb
parameters.label: Cb
parameters.description: You can modify the color of a light here by adjusting the red, green, and blue parameters.  Alternatively, clicking on the color swatch will open a dialog with HSV and/or RGB sliders allowing interact

parameters.name: wcolorb
parameters.label: Wcolorb
parameters.description: Use the R, G, and B fields to set the Component's color when displayed in wireframe shading mode.

parameters.name: lightsize2
parameters.label: Lightsize2
parameters.description: Controls the size of the source light when using Soft or Custom shadows.

parameters.name: tz
parameters.label: Tz
parameters.description: This allows you to specify the amount of movement along any of the three axes; the amount, in degrees, of rotation around any of the three axes; and a non-uniform scaling along the three axes. As an a

parameters.name: prz
parameters.label: Prz
parameters.description: Refer to the documentation on Xform page for more information.

parameters.name: pz
parameters.label: Pz
parameters.description: The Pivot point edit fields allow you to define the point about which a Component scales and rotates. Altering the pivot point of a Component produces different results depending on the transformation

parameters.name: ppz
parameters.label: Ppz
parameters.description: Refer to the documentation on Xform page for more information.

parameters.name: psz
parameters.label: Psz
parameters.description: Refer to the documentation on Xform page for more information.

parameters.name: upz
parameters.label: Upz
parameters.description: When orienting a Component, the Up Vector is used to determine where the positive Y axis points.

parameters.name: premultrgbbyalpha
parameters.label: Premultrgbbyalpha
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: shadowresolution2
parameters.label: Shadowresolution2
parameters.description: The resolution of the shadow's texture map used for the calculation.

parameters.name: orthoorigin
parameters.label: Orthoorigin
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: sz
parameters.label: Sz
parameters.description: This specifies the amount of movement along any of the three axes; the amount, in degrees, of rotation around any of the three axes; and a non-uniform scaling along the three axes. As an alternative t

parameters.name: ptz
parameters.label: Ptz
parameters.description: Refer to the documentation on Xform page for more information.

parameters.name: rz
parameters.label: Rz
parameters.description: Theis specifies the amount of movement along any of the three axes; the amount, in degrees, of rotation around any of the three axes; and a non-uniform scaling along the three axes. As an alternative

parameters.name: bgcolora
parameters.label: Bgcolora
parameters.description: Set the background color of the view when using the light as a camera.

examples.label: Set warm point light
examples.code:
l = op('light1')
l.par.lighttype = 'point'
l.par.colorr = 1.0
l.par.colorg = 0.9
l.par.colorb = 0.7
l.par.dimmer = 1.5

examples.label: Animate light intensity
examples.description: Use as expression on dimmer for a pulsing light effect.
